findings.evidence: Lines 380-384: `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` / `change s ≤ 1` against goal `⟨s,hs01⟩ ∈ Set.Ioc a₀ 1`.
findings.fix: Replace with an explicit lemma, e.g. `Subtype.coe_le_coe`/`Subtype.coe_lt_coe` (or `unitInterval.le_one`), instead of relying on defeq.
findings.issue: `change s ≤ 1` (and `change ((a₀ : I) : ℝ) < s` at line 381) close the `Set.Ioc` membership goal by unfolding the Subtype order on `I` and the `(1 : I)` coercion definitionally; line 383 has no comment, line 381's comment states what not why.

findings.evidence: Grep hits BasedPath.lean:471,481,510,889,893; `change`/`show` are flagged when used without a why-comment.
findings.fix: Document why the goal cannot be reached by `rw`/`simp only` with the relevant eval lemma, or use such a lemma instead of `change`.
findings.issue: Undocumented `change` tactics rest on beta/structure-projection defeq: line 471 (`change Continuous fun ts : I×I ↦ H ts`), 481/510 (`change endpoint (ofPath …) ∈ U`), 889/893 (`change K_fn (t,(0:I)) = (α'.trans L) 0/1`). Comments at 481/510 describe what, not why; 471/889/893 have none.

findings.evidence: Lines 153-176 define deformTerminal via `by`/`refine`; lines 182,189,195,201 each `simp [deformTerminal, …]`.
findings.fix: Give `deformTerminal` an explicit named `toFun` term and a single `deformTerminal_apply` unfolding lemma; derive the branch lemmas from it via `Path.extend` rewriting rather than re-unfolding the def each time.
findings.issue: `deformTerminal` is defined in tactic mode (`:= by … refine ⟨ContinuousMap.mk (fun t ↦ f t) …⟩` with an internal `let f := dite …`). The eval lemmas at 182/189/195 and endpoint at 201 prove their results by `simp [deformTerminal, …]`, i.e. unfolding that opaque term and reducing its nested `dite`; any reshaping of the definition breaks all four.

summary: Proofs are generally robust automation, but several goals are closed by `change` across Subtype/coercion definitional equality without justification (one with no comment), and the deformTerminal evaluation lemmas rely on simp-unfolding a tactic-constructed definition.
